message:
Last spring, I did something I'd been putting off for *months*: I printed out a simple one-page severe weather checklist and stuck it on our fridge. Sounds boring, right?

Then came the afternoon the meteorologist started talking about a derecho. My stomach sank—I *knew* we should have a plan, but my brain was already spinning. I glanced at the fridge, grabbed that little sheet, and suddenly had a north star. Charge the laptops. Fill the tubs. Bring in the porch plants. Nothing revolutionary, but having it written down meant I didn't have to *think* during the panic part.

We didn't end up needing it that badly, but the peace of mind? Totally real. Turns out a tiny bit of prep work beforehand erases so much of the "what do I do NOW?" spiral when things feel chaotic.

Do you keep a plan somewhere handy, or is that something you've been meaning to get around to too?
